critic_raw: The retrospective hospital-center evidence cannot justify mobile-van deployment because: (1) the labels are verification-biased toward referred patients, (2) the device and operator shift from hospital to mobile is untested, and (3) the referral capacity tradeoff at the relevant threshold in the mobile lower-prevalence setting is uncharacterized.
defender_raw: The QC safeguard for unreadable images and the strong aggregate performance support using this model as a starting point for a prospective pilot. The pilot design should address the concerns the critic raises — independent adjudication on all sampled patients, mobile image quality assessment, and threshold calibration at lower prevalence.

debate_rounds.critic: The defender is recommending a prospective pilot as the path forward, which agrees with my position that the current evidence is insufficient for deployment. The three issues I identified remain valid; the pilot is the appropriate remedy. I note that the QC safeguard does not resolve Issue 2 for readable-but-shifted images. Can the defender confirm that these three issues are genuine gaps in the current evidence?
debate_rounds.defender: Yes, I confirm all three issues are genuine gaps: (1) verification bias means the label quality is uncertain for screening-population cases; (2) device and operator shift creates an untested distribution difference; (3) the threshold-level referral capacity analysis at mobile lower prevalence is missing. A prospective pilot is the correct next step before deployment decisions.

adjudication_raw: The defender explicitly concedes all three issues and confirms they are genuine evidence gaps. Critique wins.
all_issues_raised: referral threshold analysis missing for mobile lower-prevalence population

empirical_test.condition: Prospective mobile-van study with independent specialist adjudication on all sampled patients, evaluating sensitivity at planned threshold in mobile lower-prevalence population
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Sensitivity below prespecified floor at planned threshold; or device shift substantially degrades performance on readable mobile images
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model meets sensitivity floor while reducing referrals materially in mobile setting
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Adequate sensitivity on readable images but high escalation rate unclear
